answer:
verdict: neither fully, but reviewer's closer to wrong here. don't gut it to 1200 — restructure it

why: for eng-brand authority, deep war stories r exactly what works. the canonical hits (cloudflare postmortems, fly.io, figma, discord migration posts) r often 2-4k words. senior engs (ur target audience 4 authority) read long if it's dense. 1200 words = generic blog post, no authority

but author's wrong that depth alone = value. 3500 words of meandering ≠ 3500 words of signal. most drafts that long have ~30% flab

what 2 actually do:
1. cut ruthlessly 4 density, not length — kill throat-clearing intro, generic context, repeated points. prob lands ~2200-2800
2. add TL;DR + strong subheads so skimmers get value in 60 sec & deep readers get the full story. this solves reviewer's real concern (attention) w/o killing depth
3. keep specifics: real numbers, wrong turns, actual configs/queries, what u'd do differently. that's the authority part — cutting those 2 hit 1200 kills the point
4. test: every section shld teach something a senior eng doesn't already know. if not, cut

so: reviewer right that draft needs big edit, wrong abt target length. author right abt depth = value, wrong that current 3500 words all earn their place
